description:
## What
Implement POST /api/v1/statuses/:id/pin (set pinned_at = now() for own post, limit 5 pins per persona, return updated Status) and DELETE /api/v1/statuses/:id/unpin (clear pinned_at, return updated Status).

## Why
Without pin/unpin actions, users cannot manage their pinned posts through clients; the feature is read-only and useless.

## Agent team pattern
- Spawn a Research subagent to: curl mastodon.social POST /api/v1/statuses/:id/pin and observe response shape, HTTP status for pinning another user's post (403), and response when pin limit exceeded; confirm limit is 5
- Spawn an Implementation subagent to: implement pin handler (verify post.persona_id == authenticated persona, count existing pins, set pinned_at, return Status); implement unpin handler (verify ownership, clear pinned_at, return Status)
- Spawn a Test subagent to: write tests for (a) successful pin returns Status with pinned=true, (b) pinning another persona's post returns 403, (c) pinning a 6th post returns 422, (d) unpin returns Status with pinned=false

## Test discipline
- Independent oracle: mastodon.social POST /api/v1/statuses/:id/pin — returns 200 Status object with pinned=true; pinning non-owned post returns 403; exceeding limit returns 422
- Test approach: use authenticated test client; verify ownership check and limit enforcement via HTTP status codes matching Mastodon reference

## Defensive programming checklist
- No `.unwrap()` in handler paths
- Verify post.persona_id == authenticated persona before any write — return 403 otherwise
- Count existing pins in same transaction as insert to avoid TOCTOU race
- Parameterized queries only — no string interpolation of IDs into SQL
- Return 422 when pin limit (5) is exceeded, matching Mastodon behavior
title: POST /statuses/:id/pin and DELETE /statuses/:id/unpin endpoints
